title: Bulk & Shipping Finance Manager
description:
Our client, a fast-growing international shipping and trading business backed by reputable investors, is looking to hire a Bulk & Shipping Finance Manager to lead the finance function and partner globally with teams in Singapore, Dubai, and Miami.


Key Responsibilities:


  • Set up core finance function: Implement accounting systems, internal controls, and reporting structures from day one.
  • Treasury operations: Establish treasury in Singapore; oversee bank account structures, liquidity management, daily cash positioning, and intercompany cash flows.
  • Cash flow forecasting: Build robust short- and medium-term cash flow models to manage working capital and support investor visibility.
  • Financial reporting: Deliver timely management accounts, variance analysis, and consolidated financial statements under IFRS.
  • Management accounting: Provide reporting on voyage economics, charter party performance, and trading desk results.
  • Hedge accounting & derivatives: Implement hedge accounting processes and ensure compliance in reporting for fuel hedges, FX exposures, and derivative contracts.
  • Audit & compliance readiness: Prepare the company for external audits and investor due diligence, ensuring all records and processes are compliant from the outset.
  • Investor reporting: Develop monthly and quarterly packs for VC investors, including P&L, balance sheet, and cash flow statements.
  • Governance & risk control: Put in place policies for authorisations, payment approvals, and financial risk management.
  • Cross-functional partnering: Support commercial heads in Singapore, Dubai, and Miami by linking financial data to operational decision-making.


Requirements:

  • Experience: 10+ years in a back-office Finance role, focusing on Treasury, accounting and reporting.
  • Sector Exposure: From a bulk shipping business or trader with a shipping arm.
  • Technical Expertise:
  1. Understanding of IFRS (especially IFRS 9 for hedge accounting, IFRS 15 for revenue recognition, IFRS 16 for leases).
  2. Voyage accounting: freight, hire, bunkers, demurrage, and port costs.
  3. Treasury management: multi-currency cash management, liquidity planning, bank facilities, and guarantees.
  4. Hedge accounting: fuel hedges, FFAs, and FX risk management.
  5. Experience with vessel financing (debt, leasing, sale & leaseback).
  6. Proficiency in financial systems/ERP, ideally with exposure to shipping platforms (e.g. IMOS/Veson).
  • Location: Singapore with potential travel requirements to Dubai and Miami.
  • Soft Skills:
  1. Hands on leadership due to the lean framework of a new business
  2. Entrepreneurial mindset, experience in a growing business.
  3. Coss cultural communication, international experience would be beneficial

postedCompany.description:
Kimberley Consulting is a leading boutique Executive Search based in Asia, specialising in human capital within specific industries including Banking and Finance, Consumer markets, FMCG, Energy and Technology, among others. 

 

With a strong network in 11 countries in the region including Singapore, China, Japan and Korea to name a few, our global network built up over 10 years in the business has enabled us to further locate talent from different parts of the world, ensuring that the candidates we source for our clients are the best to be found. 

 

Additionally, Kimberley Consulting also offers company incorporation and related consulting services to foreign-based companies looking to move into Singapore. We assist in all processes involved in the setting up of offices in Singapore, including office re-locations, managing manpower staffing needs and other services as required.
postedCompany.name: KIMBERLEY CONSULTING PTE. LTD.
